examples: Several notable researchers are studying the effects of hyperventilation.
Someone panicking can take as many as 30 breaths a minute. This is “hyperventilation”.
The effect of pursed lip inspiration and inspiratory sniffing on mean arterial pressure depended upon accompanying hyperventilation.
Some patients in the clinical trial suffered an increased heart rate and a condition called metabolic acidosis, which can lead to hyperventilation, fatigue and anorexia.
Nobody died of hyperventilation in the press.
